chunks.text: Christina Rees MP and Sir Roger Gale MP House of Commons London SW1A 0AA Wednesday 7 June 2022 Dear Christina Rees MP and Sir Roger Gale MP, We haveseentheopen letter sent to your Committee by Big Brother Watch, ARTICLE 19 Open Rights Group, Index on Censorship and Global Partners Digital . As you knowthebulk of my oral evidence on behalf of FullFact was about freedom of expression. We respect these organisations and we hope the Committee will engage with their concerns. However, with respect, we believe their letter underplays the risk to freedom of expression from the status quo. They say: "The legislation enlists social media companies to act as private online police whowillhave to adjudicate on the legality of online content and restrict whattheydeem illegal." These sorts of decisions are a lready happening. They also say: "The Bill also creates a new category of speech where the Government deems such expression to be “harmful”. Under the threat of penalties, the legislation will cause online intermediaries to censor swathes of legal online discussion including in matters of general discourse and public policy." Platforms already censor swathes of legal online discussion. As I said in evidence to the Committee, "we have normalised a level of censorship that was unimaginable in previous generations." It is up to the Committee to use the Bill to challenge and change this. We called on the Committee to amend the Bill to tackle three main threats to freedom of expression: 1. From internet companies, by legislating to require free speech based approaches to content moderation to be preferred to the current large scale content restrictions limited by guarantee and registered in England and Wales A registered charity (no. 1158683) and a non-profit company (no. 6975984) limited by guarantee and registered in England and Wales fullfact. org 2. From algorithms overreaching, by recognizing that content moderation is safety critical and legislating for independent public third party testing justaswehave for other safety critical industries to from car manufacturing to water supply 3. From our own government, by legislating to stop them pressuring internet companies to restrict certain kinds of content online astheydonow These threats exist with or without the Bill as it stands. Withdrawing the Billwilldo nothing to defend freedom of expression. The Committee has the power to amend it to protect freedom of expression. We are here towork with you to do that, and our report Tackling Online Misin for mation in an Open Society setsoutmore. Yours sincerely, WillMoy Chief Executive, FullFact CcMark Johnson - Big Brother Watch Barbora Bukovská - ARTICLE 19 Jim Killock - Open Rights Group Ruth Smeeth - Index on Censorship Richard Wingfield - Global Partners Digital https://fullfact. org/about/policy/reports/full-fact-report-2022/ limited by guarantee and registered in England and Wales A registered charity (no. 1158683) and a non-profit company (no. 6975984) limited by guarantee and registered in England and Wales fullfact. org
